Immediate Need – Production Role



Our bookkeeping team is currently at capacity.



We are hiring 1 experienced bookkeeper or CPA who can step in immediately and handle multi-year cleanup, reconciliation, and tax-ready financials without handholding.



This is a production role, not a training role.



NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENTS (READ BEFORE APPLYING)



You must:



Have real experience with U.S. bookkeeping clients

Be able to work during U.S. business hours (Central Time)

Be proficient in QuickBooks Online (QBO)

Be proactive and able to take initiative



Future flexibility may be available after proven performance



If you do not meet ALL of these, please do not apply.



About Harvest AF



Harvest AF is a U.S.-based financial consulting firm focused on:



• Tax strategy

• Bookkeeping & cleanup

• Financial clarity for business owners



We operate as a financial ministry, dedicated to serving others with integrity, excellence, and stewardship.



We believe:

When finances are structured properly, people can better serve their families, communities, and purpose.



Our work is not just about numbers — it’s about impact, responsibility, and service.



We operate with:

Urgency

Structure

Accountability



You will be working in a CPA-reviewed system, where accuracy directly impacts real people and businesses.



Your Mission



Clean up and stabilize client books so they are:



Accurate

Organized

Ready for tax preparation and planning



So our clients can steward their finances well.



Real Client Scenario (Multi-Year Cleanup Work)



You may be assigned clients with:



• 1–3 years of unclean books

• Thousands of uncategorized transactions

• Bank accounts not reconciled for multiple years

• Missing or inconsistent vendor names

• Duplicate or missing entries

• Personal and business expenses mixed together

• Disorganized Chart of Accounts



Your Responsibility in This Scenario



You will:



Clean and categorize transactions across multiple years

Standardize vendor names (no blanks)

Remove duplicates and fix errors

Reconcile accounts month-by-month until fully caught up

Separate business vs personal expenses correctly

Organize Chart of Accounts logically

Flag missing or unclear information



How We Expect You to Work



Work chronologically (month-by-month)

Reconcile as you go (not at the end)

Use QBO tools efficiently (rules, bulk upload, etc.)

Think through problems before asking questions

Communicate issues clearly



Tax Awareness & Cross-Team Collaboration (CRITICAL)



You will work closely with our tax planning and tax preparation team.



You are not expected to be a tax expert — but you must understand how bookkeeping impacts tax outcomes.



In practice, you will:



Ensure financials are accurate for tax filing

Properly record assets and major purchases

Support depreciation and expense strategies

Flag transactions that impact:



Depreciation

Section 179 / large purchases

Owner distributions

Business vs personal classification



Deliver tax-ready financials to our CPA/EA team



DO NOT APPLY IF:



• You need training on QBO

• You cannot align with U.S. working hours

• You rely on instructions for every step

• You have never handled cleanup work

• You only do basic data entry bookkeeping



IDEAL CANDIDATE



• Experienced with multi-year cleanup work

• Strong QBO user (including bulk tools)

• Detail-oriented and fast

• Thinks independently

• Communicates clearly

• Understands how bookkeeping impacts taxes



What Success Looks Like



Reconciliations tie out to the penny

Cleanup completed within 7–10 days (per phase)

Less than 1% corrections after QA

Issues identified and resolved proactively



Why Work With Us



• Long-term opportunity

• Stable workload

• CPA-reviewed system (you’ll grow fast)

• Opportunity for increased pay based on performance and experience

• Potential for more flexible schedule over time

• Faith-driven, purpose-centered team

• Work that directly impacts people and businesses



How to Apply



Subject Line:

Bookkeeper Application – [Your Name]



Include:

Your experience with U.S. bookkeeping clients

Tools you’ve used (QBO required)

Example of a cleanup project:

What was wrong

What you fixed

Final result

Your current working hours (your local time)

Confirm your ability to align with U.S. hours



REQUIRED QUESTIONS (MUST ANSWER)



1 What is the first thing you check when starting a bookkeeping cleanup and why?



2 If you are given 2 years of unclean books, what is your step-by-step approach?



3 How do you handle large purchases (equipment, vehicles, etc.), and why does it matter for taxes?



Hiring Process



• Application Review

• Paid Trial Task (real client work)

• Decision within 48 hours



We move fast.



IMPORTANT



If you are not confident handling multi-year cleanup independently, this role is not a fit.

title: Book Keeper
description:
******MUST SUBMIT A RESUME AND VOICE RECORDING TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THE ROLE************





Position Overview:



We are looking for a dependable and detail-oriented Bookkeeper to join our team and take ownership of our day-to-day financial operations. This role is essential to maintaining the accuracy, organization, and integrity of our financial records. The ideal candidate is someone who enjoys working with numbers, has a strong sense of accountability, and can manage multiple financial tasks with precision and consistency.



As a Bookkeeper, you will play a key role in supporting the financial health of the organization by ensuring that all transactions are recorded properly and that accounts are kept up to date. You will work closely with management to provide clear visibility into financial performance and help ensure that all financial processes run smoothly.



Key Responsibilities:



In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining and updating financial records, including tracking daily transactions such as income, expenses, and payments. You will handle accounts payable and receivable, ensuring invoices are issued accurately and payments are collected and recorded in a timely manner. Regular reconciliation of bank and credit card statements will be part of your routine to ensure all records align correctly.



You will also assist with payroll processing, maintain organized financial documentation, and prepare periodic financial reports that provide insight into the company’s financial position. Attention to detail is critical, as you will be expected to identify discrepancies and resolve them efficiently. Additionally, you will help ensure compliance with applicable financial regulations and internal procedures.



Qualifications and Experience:



The successful candidate will have prior experience in bookkeeping or a similar accounting role, along with a solid understanding of basic accounting principles. Familiarity with accounting software such as QuickBooks, Xero, or similar platforms is important, as well as comfort working with spreadsheets and digital financial tools.



Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize tasks are essential in this role, as you will be handling multiple responsibilities at once. You should also demonstrate a high level of discretion and professionalism when working with sensitive financial information.



While not required, a degree in accounting, finance, or a related field, as well as any bookkeeping certifications, would be considered an asset.



Work Environment and Compensation:



This position offers the opportunity to work in a collaborative and supportive environment where accuracy and reliability are highly valued. Compensation will be based on experience and qualifications, and additional benefits may be available depending on employment type.



How to Apply:



If you are a motivated and detail-focused professional looking to contribute to a growing organization, we encourage you to apply. Please submit your resume along with a brief cover letter describing your relevant experience and interest in the role to [Insert
 or application link].

title: Project Managers (5x open roles)
description:
Here's the updated version with the new intro framing — 5 distinct PM roles, each owning a specific team:



---



**Project Manager — 5 Open Roles (Philippines, Remote)**



We're hiring 5 Project Managers to run point on execution across our multi-brand operation. Each role owns a specific team and a specific outcome. These are leadership roles — you'll direct people, track deliverables, hold teammates accountable, and drive work across the finish line on schedule.



If you're someone who naturally organizes chaos, runs tight project boards, and gets energy from shipping work on time — keep reading.



**The 5 Roles**



*Role 1 — Creative Team PM*

Run our creative output engine. Manage the creative database, review work, give feedback, track weekly deliverables, and report on results. Own the execution calendar for ads, graphics, and static creative. You make sure creative ships on time and hits the mark.



*Role 2 — App Building Team PM*

Manage app-building projects across the portfolio. Give feedback, track builds, and ship product. Hands-on capability expected — you should be comfortable building in Lovable, directing AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT), and actively moving projects forward yourself, not just coordinating.



*Role 3 — Social Content Team PM*

Run our social creation team. Manage the content database, review output, give feedback, track weekly deliverables, and track performance. Own execution across AI content, b-roll, and long-form YouTube. You keep the content machine producing on schedule.



*Role 4 — Notion PMT Manager*

Own our Notion project management tooling. Keep it dialed in, clean, and current. Make sure automations and reporting systems are running correctly, and continuously find ways to improve workflows so the team stays organized and focused. You're the connective tissue that keeps everything visible.



*Role 5 — Launch PM (Carnivore Bar Everyday Bar Presale)*

Drive the end-to-end launch of our Everyday Bar presale and funnels. Coordinate across marketing, creative, and ops. Request work from multiple teams, hold them accountable to deadlines, and keep everything tracked in a single Notion project area. Daily accountability on execution. You make this launch a success.



**What All 5 Roles Have In Common**



- You run a team or a project, not a task list

- You track deliverables in Notion and hold people to deadlines

- You communicate clearly, request work cleanly, and follow up without friction

- You own outcomes — not just activity



**Growth Path**



Strong performers grow into:

- Head of Remote Ops

- Operations Director

- Brand Operator (running a specific brand end-to-end)

- Chief of Staff



We promote from within based on performance and ownership.



**Who We're Looking For**



- 2+ years managing projects, teams, or remote operations

- Strong written English and clear, concise communication

- Naturally organized — you think in timelines, owners, and next steps

- Comfortable holding people accountable without creating tension

- Able to work across time zones with a US-based founder and team

- Self-directed — you move work forward without being told what to do next



*Preferred (varies by role)*

- Notion proficiency (databases, views, automations)

- Shopify, Amazon, or DTC e-commerce background

- Familiarity with AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Lovable, Zapier, Make)

- Creative ops or content production experience



**Who This Is For**



Someone who wants to run a team, not just complete tasks. Someone who takes pride in things getting done on time and done well. Someone who wants to grow into a senior operations role inside a fast-moving, AI-native company.



If that's you, tell us which of the 5 roles fits you best and apply.

title: SEO & Affiliate Operations Manager — Gold IRA Niche (Attorney-Led US Site)
description:
SEO & Affiliate Operations Manager — Gold IRA Niche (Attorney-Led US Site)



Salary: $650/month starting, with clear path to $1,200+/month based on performance

Performance Bonus: Up to $200/month additional based on KPIs (traffic, rankings, conversions)

Hours: 8 hours/day, Monday to Friday

Time Zone: Flexible, but 2–3 hours overlap with Israel time (GMT+3) required

Long-term: This is a long-term role. We are building a 7-figure business and want someone who grows with us.





ABOUT US



We are Elite Asset Shield (
), a US-focused affiliate content site in the Gold IRA and Crypto IRA niche. The site is led by a licensed attorney specializing in international asset protection. Our target audience is US investors aged 50+ with significant retirement assets.



We are entering an aggressive growth phase with a clear 12–18 month roadmap to $1M ARR through organic SEO,
, and YouTube. We have the strategy, budget, and systems. We need an execution partner.





WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR



You are NOT a general VA. You are a specialist who has done this before. If you have not personally managed the growth of an affiliate or content site, please do not apply.



You have:



- 2+ years of hands-on SEO experience, ideally in finance, legal, or other YMYL niches

- Proven portfolio of websites you have helped grow (we will ask for URLs and specific metrics)

- Strong English writing and editing skills (American English)

- Working knowledge of: WordPress, Rank Math or Yoast, Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs or SEMrush, Surfer SEO

- Experience with
 marketing platforms (Beehiiv, ConvertKit, or similar)

- Experience coordinating with freelance writers (hiring, briefing, editing, paying)

- Experience with backlink outreach: HARO/Connectively, guest posts, digital PR

- Excellent organizational skills — you can run ClickUp or Notion boards and produce weekly reports without being asked twice



Bonus points for:



- Direct experience in Gold IRA, precious metals, or retirement finance niches

- YouTube channel operations experience

- Basic HTML/CSS for WordPress tweaks

- Canva or basic Photoshop for featured images





WHAT YOU WILL DO



Weekly responsibilities:



- Manage the editorial pipeline: brief writers, review drafts, run SEO optimization in Surfer, publish to WordPress

- Coordinate 10–15 HARO/Connectively submissions per week (attorney writes responses, you handle dispatch and tracking)

- Send 10+ guest post pitches per week to DR 40+ finance sites

- Manage weekly newsletter: draft in our voice, get attorney approval, schedule, track performance

- Monitor Google Search Console for ranking changes, opportunities, and issues

- Produce a Monday morning weekly report using our exact format (provided)

- Respond to reader
s using approved templates

- Track all affiliate conversions across 8+ programs and reconcile against analytics



Monthly responsibilities:



- Hire and onboard new writers as we scale (2–4 writers to manage by month 3)

- Run monthly technical SEO audit

- Update and maintain the comparison pages and review articles as offers change

- Identify 3–5 new keyword opportunities from competitor analysis





WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE



Month 3:

- 30+ high-quality articles published

- 500+
 subscribers

- 10+ backlinks from DR 40+ sites



Month 6:

- 60+ articles ranking in top 50

- 3,000+
 subscribers

- First $5K+ month in affiliate revenue



Month 12:

- Site traffic above 50K monthly sessions

- 15,000+
 subscribers

- Monthly revenue above $30K



When we hit these milestones, your compensation scales. This is not a vague promise — we will put the specific triggers in writing in your contract.





HOW TO APPLY



Applications that do not follow this format will be ignored. We are looking for someone who reads carefully and follows instructions — prove that in your application.



Send us:



1. A 5-minute Loom video introducing yourself. Tell us: (a) your name, (b) one website you helped grow and the specific results you achieved, (c) why you think you are a fit for this role, (d) one thing you noticed about 
 that you would change immediately.



2. A written answer to these three questions (maximum 3 sentences each):

   • What is your current experience with YMYL or finance-niche SEO?

   • Walk us through how you would choose between publishing 5 long-form articles vs 15 short articles per month.

   • What is the difference between Goldco and Augusta Precious Metals, and which would you prioritize in a comparison article? (You are allowed to research — we want to see how you think, not what you already know.)



3. Links to 2–3 live websites you have worked on, with a one-sentence description of your contribution.



4. Your preferred start date and confirmation that you can commit to 8 hours per day, 5 days per week, for at least 6 months.



5. In your application subject line, write: "EAS-VA Application — [Your Name] — [Number of years of SEO experience]"



We review applications within 5 business days. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 30-minute video interview, followed by a paid 5-day trial period at full rate.





FINAL NOTE



We are serious operators building a serious business. We value initiative, honesty, and follow-through over credentials. If you are hungry, fast, and take ownership — even if your resume is not perfect — we want to hear from you.



If you are looking for easy work, passive hours, or something you can do while managing five other clients, this is not the role for you.



We are hiring ONE person for this role. Take your time with your application.

title: Meta Ads Creator. ai UGC ads
description:
Here's a job description you can use:



---



**Meta Ads Creative Producer (Freelance / Part-time)**



We're looking for a creative ad producer to design and edit short-form video ads for our
 and Instagram (Meta) campaigns. You won't be handling strategy, targeting, or campaign management — your focus is purely on producing scroll-stopping creatives.



**What you'll do**



Using our existing product videos and product photos, you'll build unique video ads that follow a proven structure:



- **0–3 sec** — Hard hook introducing a product problem and the solution

- **3–10 sec** — Product showcase

- **10–15 sec** — Website preview

- **15–25 sec** — Product showcase with a strong selling hook



You'll produce multiple versions of each ad in different formats, angles, and styles so we can test what performs best. Expect to deliver a steady stream of fresh creatives each week.



**What you bring**



- Hands-on experience with CapCut (or equivalent editing software)

- Familiarity with AI text-to-video tools like Kling, Runway, or similar

- A strong eye for pacing, hooks, and short-form content that converts

- A portfolio or examples of ad creatives you've made — ideally for e-commerce or DTC brands

- Ability to work independently, iterate quickly, and deliver on deadline



**Nice to have**



- Understanding of what works on Meta (trends, winning ad formats, UGC style)

title: Telemarketer / Lead Generation Specialist
description:
**About Us:**

Workforce Connect is a staffing and workforce management company specializing in providing reliable housekeeping staff, cleaners, banquet staff, kitchen helpers, and other hospitality workers to hotels and facilities across Canada. We help hotels solve staffing shortages quickly and efficiently.



**Job Summary:**

We are looking for a motivated and results-driven Telemarketer / Lead Generation Specialist to help expand our client base by reaching out to hotels, property managers, and hospitality businesses. Your main responsibility will be generating qualified leads, identifying decision-makers, and setting appointments for our management team.



**Responsibilities:**



* Contact hotels, motels, resorts, and property management companies

* Introduce Workforce Connect staffing services

* Identify decision-makers such as General Managers, HR Managers, and Operations Managers

* Generate qualified leads and update CRM records

* Schedule meetings or discovery calls for the sales team

* Follow up with warm leads and previous inquiries

* Maintain accurate records of calls, responses, and lead status

* Meet weekly and monthly lead generation targets



**Requirements:**



* Previous experience in telemarketing, lead generation, sales, or customer service preferred

* Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)

* Confident and professional phone manner

* Experience using CRM systems is an advantage

* Familiarity with hotel or hospitality industry is a plus

* Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment for remote work

* Self-motivated and target-driven mindset



**Compensation:**



* Competitive base pay + performance bonuses

* Commission incentives for qualified leads and closed clients

* Long-term growth opportunity within the company



**Why Join Us:**



* Work remotely

* Performance-based earning potential

* Growing company with expansion opportunities

* Supportive and fast-paced environment



**How to Apply:**



Please send the following:



* Resume

* Short intro video explaining what you think about the role (**No script reading**)

* OnlineJobs profile link

* Portfolio (optional)



Apply here:





Join Workforce Connect and help us build stronger hotel partnerships across Canada.

title: Community Growth & Social Media Marketing Specialist
description:
I'm the founder of VidaEase, a web platform that helps English-speaking expats navigate Spain's complex visa and residency processes — NIE applications, Digital Nomad Visa, Non-Lucrative Visa, and more. We're a Delaware LLC launching our MVP in May 2026. I work 15 hours/week on the business and need a marketing-minded partner to own community growth and content promotion.



This is not a general VA role. I'm looking for someone who thinks like a marketer — someone who can grow a
 community from 0 to 
, get organic traction in
 groups, manage an SEO content pipeline, and run
 campaigns that actually convert. You'll have real ownership over channels, not just task lists.



What you'll do every day (4 hrs/day):

Community Growth, Engagement & Intelligence (~1.5 hrs/day)



Monitor 10–
 expat
 groups daily. Draft comment and post scripts for me to publish from my personal account 
 groups require personal accounts — you draft, I post).

Grow and engage our
 community: daily tips, respond to members, welcome new joiners, coordinate weekly Q&A sessions.

Track which posts, threads, and groups drive the most waitlist signups and paid conversions.

Expat Pain Point Monitoring: As you read group discussions daily, log recurring pain points, frustrations, and questions expats have about the NIE, Digital Nomad Visa, and Non-Lucrative Visa processes. Compile a weekly "Pain Points & Trends" report: what problems keep coming up, what workarounds people are sharing, and what's causing the most confusion. This directly shapes our product content and checklist updates.

Immigration Law Change Monitoring: Track English-language Spain expat news sources (Expatica, The Local Spain, key
 group admin posts, government announcements) for changes to Spanish immigration law, visa requirements, processing times, or consulate procedures that affect NIE, DNV, or NLV applicants. Flag anything urgent to me immediately. Include a "Regulatory Changes" section in your weekly report with source links.



Content Publishing & SEO (~1 hr/day)



Format and publish 2 SEO blog articles per week (I write drafts). You handle: meta titles, descriptions, internal links, header tags, keyword placement, images, and CTA formatting.

Schedule and manage social media content across Instagram, LinkedIn, Pinterest, and our
 Business Page using Buffer.

Create social graphics in Canva.



Outreach & Promotion (~1 hr/day)



Research and contact potential partners: immigration law firms, health insurance affiliates, YouTube creators, and expat media outlets.

Send outreach
s using approved templates. Manage the full provider pipeline in our Master Tracker (Google Sheets).

Coordinate YouTube creator affiliate partnerships (25–30% recurring commission model).

Draft and send press outreach to Spain expat media outlets.



 Marketing & Analytics (~30 min/day)



Manage Mailchimp campaigns: Founding Member sequences, subscriber onboarding, waitlist nurture
s, and launch announcements.

Update the Master Tracker daily:
 list growth,
 members, Founding Member sales, articles published, provider contacts, community engagement metrics.

Compile a weekly report for my review that includes: KPI summary, Pain Points & Trends digest (top recurring issues from community monitoring), and a Regulatory Changes section (any immigration law or process updates flagged that week with source links).



What I'm looking for:



2+ years of experience in social media marketing, community management, or digital marketing

Strong English writing skills — you'll be drafting copy that sounds like a real person, not a brand

Experience with
 group engagement (understanding group culture, rules, organic value-first posting)

Good research instincts — able to spot patterns in community conversations and summarize them clearly (e.g., "this week 12 people asked about NIE appointment delays in Valencia")

Familiar with SEO basics: keyword placement, meta descriptions, internal linking, on-page optimization

Experience with Mailchimp (or similar
 platform) — list management, sequences, basic analytics

Comfortable with: Buffer, Canva, Google Sheets, Google Analytics

Self-starter who can own a channel, not just follow a checklist. I'll give you strategy and direction — you figure out the best way to execute.

Available to work 4 hours/day, Monday–Friday. Flexible on which 4 hours, but consistent daily schedule preferred.



Nice to have (not required):



Experience growing a
 community or 
 server

Familiarity with the expat, immigration, or digital nomad niche

Experience monitoring regulatory or policy changes and summarizing them for a non-specialist audience

Experience with affiliate marketing or creator partnerships

Basic understanding of Stripe or subscription business metrics



About working with me:



I'm a solo founder building this as a side project (15 hrs/week). I handle all strategy, writing, developer management, and Spanish-language provider calls. You'll communicate with me daily via a short morning check-in message (Slack or
) — what you did yesterday, what you're doing today, any blockers. I review and approve content weekly, not for every post. I trust you to execute within the brand voice and strategy we agree on. I pay biweekly, on time, every time. No micromanagement. If you're good, this becomes a long-term role that grows with the business.



To apply:

Please include in your application:



A brief description of a community or social media channel you've grown (numbers, timeframe, what you did).

A link to a
 group post or social media campaign you're proud of.

Your daily availability (which 4-hour block works best for you).



I'll respond to qualified applications within 48 hours. Shortlisted candidates will do a short paid test task before hiring. The role starts with a 2-week paid trial at full rate so we can both make sure the fit is right, then continues as an ongoing position that grows with the business.

title: Global Virtual Assistant
description:
POSITION SUMMARY

This role is responsible for managing schedules, coordinating team workflows, supporting meetings and events, and ensuring day-to-day administrative functions run smoothly. The ideal candidate is organized, proactive, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities in a professional environment.



This position is 100% remote.



ABOUT CARDONE VENTURES

Our mission is to help business owners achieve their personal, professional, and financial goals through the growth of their businesses. We work in dozens of verticals and provide strategic business guidance through courses, live events, partnerships, and investments. Our core values are the backbone of our business and guide our hiring process: we are inspirational, accountable, transparent, disciplined, aligned, and results-oriented. This company operates nationally and is growing by the day.



Wondering what it's like to work here? Come check this:


/watch?v=PIIv7sYj1Ns

· Support special projects and additional administrative tasks as assigned.



COMPETENCIES

· Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.

· Ability to handle confidential information with professionalism.

· Proficiency with calendar,
, and standard office software tools.

· Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

• Prolonged periods sitting at a desk and working on a computer



COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY

As an equal opportunity employer committed to meeting the needs of a multigenerational and multicultural workforce, Cardone Ventures recognizes that a diverse staff, reflective of our community, is an integral and welcome part of a successful and ethical business. We hire local talent at all levels regardless of race, color, religion, age, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, or disability, and actively foster inclusion in all forms both within our company and across interactions with clients, candidates, and partners.



If this position caught your eye, send us your resume! For best consideration, please complete the online application form:

title: Customer Support Manager for Shopify Fashion Ecommerce (Part-Time, Long-Term, Remote)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



This is a long-term part-time role for an experienced Customer Support Manager. We are not looking for someone for one month. We want someone we can grow with, give more responsibility to, and pay more over time as the team scales.



You will NOT be answering tickets. You will be managing the people who do.



We already have a clear structure in place: written SOPs, proven support funnels, macros, and defined workflows that we continue to optimize. We are NOT hiring you to redesign or rebuild our customer support process. We are hiring you to make sure the structure we have is followed consistently, every day, by every CSR on the team.



Your job is to make sure our customer support team handles every ticket according to the SOPs and macros, our refund rate stays low, and our KPIs stay healthy. You will audit ticket samples daily, track team performance against our SOP, write coaching feedback, and report weekly to the company directors with a clear summary of what is working, what is not, and what needs to change.



You will be managing a team of 3 to 4 CSRs. Part of your role is active two-way communication: coaching and feedback downward to the CSRs when they deviate from SOP, and clear reporting upward to the directors on team performance (FRT, Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance) and where the biggest opportunities for improvement sit. You are the link between the team and leadership.



-------------------------



WHAT YOU WILL DO DAILY (2 to 3 hours per day, 7 days per week)



- Audit a sample of Gorgias tickets handled by our 3 to 4 CSRs against our SOP and macros

- Score CSRs on SOP compliance, tone, refund handling, and escalation judgment

- Write short coaching notes when CSRs deviate from SOP, and follow up to confirm the behavior changed

- Track KPIs: First Response Time (FRT), Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance percentage

- Communicate directly with CSRs on performance, corrections, and questions about the workflow

- Run the weekly Risky Products Report process (every Monday before 12:00 PHT), chasing CS and Disputes for input and escalating to the product team

- Send a weekly KPI and insights report to the company directors, highlighting team performance and where the opportunities for improvement sit

- Flag patterns: which products drive refunds, which CSRs need coaching, where the SOP has gaps



-------------------------



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT



- This is not a role to rebuild or redesign the customer support process. The structure, funnels, SOPs, and macros are already in place and working.

- This is not a ticket-handling role. If you are looking for hands-on CSR work, this is not a fit.

- This is not a role for someone who wants to "make exceptions to be nice." We enforce process.



-------------------------



SCHEDULE. READ THIS BEFORE APPLYING



This role is 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day. Customer support runs every day, so quality oversight runs every day. If you cannot commit to working 7 days a week, please do not apply. We will not make exceptions, and applying anyway only wastes both our time. You can choose your hours within the day, but you must check in 7 days a week.



-------------------------



WHO YOU ARE



- You have hands-on, provable experience MANAGING a customer support team in ecommerce. Not just doing the work yourself, but auditing others, coaching, and reporting up. Direct CSM experience, OR senior CS team lead experience, OR CS QA / Quality Analyst experience all count (any combination works). Be prepared to show concrete examples in round 2.

- You have used Gorgias before. This is not negotiable for this role.

- You have worked with Shopify ecommerce stores.

- Bonus: experience in fashion ecommerce specifically.

- You are comfortable working with numbers. Pivot tables, percentages, refund rates, KPI dashboards. You do not need to be an analyst, but you cannot be afraid of a spreadsheet.

- Your written English is professional and precise. You can write coaching feedback that is direct without being rude, and reports that a busy executive can read in two minutes.

- You are a process person. You believe SOPs exist for a reason and you enforce them.



-------------------------



WHAT WE OFFER



- $6 to $8 USD per hour base, depending on proven experience

- Long-term role. We want to grow with the right person and increase pay and responsibility over time

- A fully built support operation: written SOPs, macros, proven funnels, and a defined workflow. You will not be guessing what the standards are, and you will not be asked to build them from scratch

- A direct line to the company directors. Your reports go straight up, no middle layer



-------------------------



HOW TO APPLY. READ CAREFULLY



We will only review applications that follow these instructions exactly. This is the first thing we evaluate.



1. Put the word PRECISION in the subject line of your application.



2. In your message, answer all four of these questions inline (not in an attached file):



   Q1. How long have you been managing or auditing a customer support team in ecommerce, and at what kind of company (size, niche, ticket volume, number of CSRs you oversaw)? Name the actual employer if you can.



   Q2. Describe one specific time you identified that a customer support agent was handling tickets incorrectly. What was the issue, how did you find it, how did you coach the agent, and did the behavior actually change afterwards?



   Q3. Have you used Gorgias? For how long, and at what ticket volume? If yes, name one Gorgias feature you use regularly that most people do not know about.



   Q4. Confirm you can commit to 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day, long-term. List the working hours you would propose in your local timezone.



3. State your hourly rate in USD.



Applications missing the keyword PRECISION, or that skip any of the four questions, will not be reviewed. Loom videos, CVs, and portfolio links are not needed at this stage. Please do not send them. We will request them in round 2 from shortlisted candidates.

title: CS Team Lead / QA Manager for Shopify Ecommerce (Part-Time, Remote, Long-Term)
description:
THE ROLE



Part-time Customer Support Manager to oversee a small team of 3 to 4 CSRs in our Shopify fashion ecommerce business. You will not answer tickets. You will make sure the people who do, do it correctly.



Our SOPs, macros, and support funnels are already built and working. We need someone to enforce them: audit tickets daily, coach CSRs, track KPIs (FRT, Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance), and report weekly to the directors.

-------------------------



7 DAYS A WEEK. READ THIS



This role is 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day. If you cannot do 7 days, please do not apply. No exceptions.



-------------------------



REQUIREMENTS



- Proven experience managing or QA-auditing a CS team in ecommerce (be ready to show examples)

- Hands-on Gorgias experience, not negotiable

- Process-driven. You enforce SOPs, you do not make exceptions



Bonus: fashion ecommerce experience.



-------------------------



PAY



$6 to $8 USD per hour, based on proven experience. Long-term, with pay and responsibility growing as the team scales.



-------------------------



HOW TO APPLY



We only review applications that follow these instructions exactly.



1. Put the word PRECISION in the subject line.



2. Answer all four questions inline in your message (no attachments):



   Q1. How long have you managed or audited a CS team in ecommerce? What company, what niche, what ticket volume, and how many CSRs did you oversee? Name the employer if possible.



   Q2. Describe one specific time you caught a CSR handling tickets incorrectly. How did you find it, how did you coach them, and did the behavior change?



   Q3. How long have you used Gorgias, and at what ticket volume? Name one Gorgias feature most people do not know about.



   Q4. Confirm you can commit to 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day, long-term. List your proposed working hours in your local timezone.



3. State your hourly rate in USD.



Missing the PRECISION keyword or skipping questions = we do not review. No Loom, CV, or portfolio needed yet. We request those in round 2.

title: Copy Director
description:
Circle of Greatness is seeking a highly strategic and performance-driven Copy Director to lead all copy across our
, SMS, funnel, and website marketing ecosystem.



This role is responsible for owning messaging across the entire customer journey — from first touchpoint to conversion. You will manage and mentor junior copywriters, collaborate directly with partners, and ensure every piece of communication aligns with both brand voice and revenue goals.



The ideal candidate is both creative and analytical — someone who understands how to craft compelling messaging and optimize it across funnels, campaigns, and web experiences.



Key Responsibilities

Copy Leadership & Strategy



Own and oversee all copy across
, SMS, funnels, and website pages

Define messaging frameworks, campaign angles, and conversion strategies

Guide and develop junior copywriters on tone, structure, and execution

Set clear standards for what “high-performing” copy looks like





Quality Control



Review, edit, and approve all outgoing copy across campaigns and web assets

Ensure consistency in brand voice across all platforms and partners

Maintain a high bar for clarity, persuasion, and conversion effectiveness





Performance & Optimization



Analyze performance across campaigns and funnels (open rates, CTR, conversions, revenue)

Identify winning messaging patterns and scale them

Continuously test, iterate, and optimize copy across
, SMS, and web pages

Use data to inform creative and strategic decisions





Client & Partner Management



Act as the primary copy lead for 4–5 key partners

Collaborate on campaign, funnel, and offer strategy

Ensure all deliverables align with partner goals and expectations





Campaign & Funnel Execution



Work closely with junior copywriters to produce high-quality campaigns and funnel assets

Oversee copy for landing pages, sales pages, and key website touchpoints

Support launches for promotions, events, and marketing initiatives

Ensure all copy is delivered accurately and on time





Requirements



Proven experience in copywriting and managing copy teams, preferably in digital marketing

Strong expertise in
, SMS, funnel, and website copywriting

Track record of writing or directing copy that drives measurable results (revenue, conversions)

Experience mentoring or managing junior tea
mbers

Ability to balance creative direction with data-driven decision-making

Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

Nice to Have

Experience managing multiple brands or partners simultaneously

What Success Looks Like

Junior copywriters are producing higher-quality, conversion-focused work

Copy becomes a core driver of revenue growth for Circle of Greatness

title: Operations Director
description:
OPERATIONS DIRECTOR ROLE



Employee Retention Benefits, Inc. (ERB)



1. POSITION INFORMATION



Position Title: Operations Director 



Department: Operations / Client Experience 



Reports To: Integrator 



Location: Remote / Offshore (Philippines or equivalent) 







2. PURPOSE OF THE ROLE

The Operations Director is responsible for building, leading, and stabilizing ERB’s operational engine, with a primary focus on Client Service execution, internal workflows, and operational scalability.



This role ensures that all internal processes are structured, efficient, and consistently executed to support company growth.



The Operations Director owns how work gets done, ensuring that service delivery is predictable, scalable, and free from operational friction.



This is a builder and stabilizer role, not a maintenance role.



3. ROLE DEFINITION



Operations Director



Role Purpose 

The Operations Director owns Client Service (CST) execution, QA & Audit oversight (Public Sector), and internal workflow design. This role ensures that all operational processes are standardized, documented, and scalable.



The role is responsible for aligning execution across departments and ensuring that internal delivery meets company standards for accuracy, timeliness, and efficiency.



4. CORE AREAS OF OWNERSHIP



Client Service (CST execution & workflow) 

QA & Audit (Public Sector only) 

Operational capacity planning 

SOPs, systems, and documentation 

Cross-department delivery alignment 





5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



A. Operational Strategy & Execution



Design and own end-to-end operational workflows across Client Service and internal processes 

Translate leadership priorities into structured, executable workflows 

Ensure operational readiness to support company growth and increasing demand 

Identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and breakdowns — and implement permanent solutions 

B. QA & Audit Oversight (Public Sector Only)



Establish and enforce QA standards and audit checkpoints 

Own: 

defect prevention 

error tracking 

root-cause resolution 

Ensure compliance with internal SOPs and data accuracy standards 

Provide QA reporting and trend analysis to leadership 

Note: QA & Audit for Private Sector accounts remains under Account Director.



C. Client Service (CST) Execution Oversight



Own CST workflow structure and execution standards 

Ensure CST workflows align with account and enrollment requirements 

Maintain: 

SLA performance

service quality standards

escalation workflows

title: Senior Accountant (Australian Tax and Business Services)
description:
Australian firm | Full-time role



About Create Financial

Create Financial is a modern Australian tax and advisory firm founded in 2025, built for Australia’s innovation ecosystem.



We work with founders and high-growth businesses across their entire journey — from idea -> growth -> scale -> exit.



Our services span:

- Tax and compliance

- ASIC and company secretarial

- R&D Tax Incentive

- CFO and commercial advisory



The Opportunity

This is a newly created role to support our growing client base. You’ll work directly with the Managing Partner and get exposure well beyond a traditional accounting firm role.



This role is ideal for a newly qualified CA or CPA coming out of an Australian business services or tax environment who wants something more flexible, commercial, and interesting.



It’s particularly well suited to someone returning to the workforce (e.g. a parent) who still wants to do high-quality work — without the rigid structure of a traditional firm.



You’ll get exposure to startups, founders, and real commercial decision-making — not just compliance work.



What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll support both client delivery and internal operations across the firm, including:



Client Accounting & Compliance

- Month-end support and financial reporting for clients

- Bank, payroll, and balance sheet reconciliations (Xero)

- Preparation of BAS, IAS, and income tax returns

- Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) returns



Advisory & Operations Support

- Assist with entity setup and structuring

- Prepare ASIC forms and support ongoing compliance

- Support CFO/advisory engagements (cash flow, reporting, insights)



Growth & Learning

- Assist with R&D Tax Incentive claims and documentation

- Work with startups and scale-ups across different industries

- Exposure to grants, funding, and investor readiness



What We’re Looking For

- CA or CPA qualified (or very close to completion)

- 3–6 years’ experience in Australian business services / tax

- Strong working knowledge of Xero (non-negotiable)

- Experience preparing BAS, tax returns, and reconciliations

- Comfortable working directly with clients



How You Work

- Detail-oriented but pragmatic

- Comfortable in a fast-moving, less structured environment

- Proactive and able to manage your own time

- Enjoy working closely with founders and small teams

- Curious — you want to learn beyond compliance



Why Join Create Financial

- Flexibility first — part-time hours that fit around your life

- Remote-friendly — work from anywhere

- High-impact work — direct exposure to founders and decision-making

- Variety — no two clients or days are the same

- Growth — learn CFO, advisory, and R&D (not just tax)

- Small practice — no bureaucracy, real ownership

title: Scribe for Mental Health Practice
description:
Job Title: Full-Time Virtual Medical Scribe & Administrative Assistant (Philippines-Based)

We are seeking a dedicated, full-time Virtual Medical Scribe / Administrative Assistant based in the Philippines to support a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner (PMHNP) in a busy U.S. practice.

Position Overview?This is a full-time remote position focused primarily on medical scribing for psychiatric and mental health patient encounters. You will use Freed AI (ambient AI scribe technology) and the Tebra EHR/practice management platform to create accurate, timely clinical documentation. Additional administrative support will be required as needed, where a clear, professional American English accent is a strong advantage.

Key Responsibilities

	•	Listen to and scribe patient sessions in real-time or shortly after using Freed AI to generate detailed clinical notes (SOAP notes, progress notes, psychiatric evaluations, etc.).

	•	Review, edit, and finalize documentation directly in the Tebra platform.

	•	Ensure all notes are accurate, compliant with HIPAA, and completed promptly.

	•	Handle additional administrative duties as assigned, such as scheduling, patient communications, phone calls, follow-ups, prior authorizations, or other office tasks.

	•	Collaborate closely with the PMHNP via virtual tools (Zoom, messaging platforms, etc.).

Requirements

	•	Based in the Philippines with reliable high-speed internet and a quiet, professional workspace.

	•	Proven experience as a medical scribe, virtual medical assistant, or in clinical documentation/transcription (mental health or psychiatry experience is a big plus).

	•	Strong, hands-on familiarity with Freed AI and Tebra is required — you must be comfortable using both tools daily.

	•	Excellent written and spoken English, with strong attention to detail and solid knowledge of medical terminology (especially psychiatric/mental health terms).

	•	Clear, professional American English accent is highly preferred for phone calls and other voice-based administrative duties.

	•	Full-time availability (approximately 40 hours/week) aligned with Alaska time zone business hours (typically evening/night shifts in the Philippines).

	•	Professional, reliable, proactive, and able to work independently while maintaining patient confidentiality.

	•	Computer setup suitable for healthcare documentation.

What We Offer

	•	Full-time, long-term position with consistent hours.

	•	Competitive compensation based on experience.

	•	Opportunity to support meaningful mental health care using advanced AI tools.

	•	Training and ongoing support provided.

If you are detail-oriented, tech-savvy, and experienced with Freed AI and Tebra, we would love to hear from you!

To Apply:?Please send your resume, a short cover letter highlighting your experience with Freed AI and Tebra, and a brief voice sample (30–60 seconds) demonstrating your American English accent.
salary: $6.00 HR and Up Depending Upon Experienc

title: Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Care & Financial Management Services
description:
Experienced Home Health Care Biller

Robland Home Health Care & Aspen FMS



Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Health Care & FMS

Employer: Robland Home Health Care / Aspen FMS

Experience Required: Must be already trained and experienced in home health care billing

Work Hours: Monday through Friday, business hours (Central Time)

Work Type: Remote / Virtual

Role Focus: Full-cycle billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and MCOs



About Us

Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS provide in-home care and Financial Management Services (FMS) to Minnesotans enrolled in programs administered through the Minnesota Department of Human Services (MnDHS). Accurate, on-time billing is how our caregivers get paid and how the company stays healthy. We’re hiring an experienced biller to take full ownership of this function.

Role Overview

This is not an entry-level or trainee role. We are looking for a biller who already knows home health care billing inside and out — specifically billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and managed care organizations (MCOs). You should be able to hit the ground running: submitting claims in MN–ITS, verifying authorizations, reconciling remittance advices, and working denials without step-by-step hand-holding.

You will own the full billing cycle for both Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS. This role reports directly to leadership and works closely with intake, scheduling, and payroll. Because billing accuracy drives revenue and compliance, this is one of the most trusted seats in the company.

Key Responsibilities

Claim Submission

• Submit clean claims to MN–ITS, MA, waivered services, and applicable MCOs on a consistent weekly / bi-weekly cycle.

• Verify that each claim matches the service authorization, documented hours, correct service codes and modifiers, and participant eligibility for the date(s) of service.

• Stay ahead of MnDHS timely-filing deadlines — no missed windows.

• Maintain organized, auditable billing records for every claim submitted.

Authorization & Eligibility

• Verify participant eligibility and active service authorizations before billing.

• Track authorization units, start/end dates, and remaining balances; flag authorizations about to expire or run out.

• Coordinate with intake to ensure new authorizations are captured and entered before services are billed.

Denials, Rejections & AR Follow-up

• Read and reconcile remittance advices (RAs / 835s); identify denials, rejections, and underpayments.

• Diagnose root causes — auth mismatches, documentation gaps, coding issues, eligibility lapses — correct and resubmit within filing windows.

• Maintain a denial log, surface trends, and fix recurring issues at the source rather than re-billing the same problem.

• Keep accounts receivable clean and well-aged; nothing slips past recoverable windows.

Compliance & Documentation

• Ensure all billed services are supported by compliant documentation (timesheets, visit notes, EVV records, care plans) before submission.

• Follow MnDHS billing guidelines, MA policies, and FMS program rules exactly as written.

• Maintain strict HIPAA compliance and confidentiality on all participant, employee, and payer information.

• Pull records quickly for internal or external audits.

Reporting & Communication

• Provide weekly billing reports to leadership: claims submitted, paid, pending, denied, and aging AR.

• Flag revenue risks early — missed windows, repeated denials, documentation gaps, auth issues.

• Communicate clearly with intake, scheduling, and payroll when billing issues trace back to their workflows.

• Respond to MnDHS, MCO, and payer inquiries professionally and on time.

Required Qualifications

• Home Health Care Billing Experience: 2+ years of hands-on billing experience in home health care, home care, PCA, CFSS, waivered services, or a similar Minnesota-based care setting. This is non-negotiable — applicants without direct home care billing experience will not be considered.

• MnDHS / MN–ITS Proficiency: Direct experience submitting claims through MN–ITS under MnDHS guidelines. You know the system, the service codes, and the rules without needing to be taught them.

• Payer Knowledge: Working knowledge of Medical Assistance (MA), waiver programs, and Minnesota managed care organizations (UCare, Blue Plus, Medica, HealthPartners, etc.).

• Denial Management: Proven ability to read 835s / remittance advices, diagnose denials, and rework claims within timely-filing windows.

• EVV Familiarity: Understands Electronic Visit Verification and how EVV data flows into claims.

• Detail-Oriented: Obsessive accuracy with numbers, codes, dates, and documentation. Small errors create large problems in this role.

• Self-Directed: Able to own the billing function independently, set your own cadence, and hit deadlines without being managed minute-to-minute.

• Technology: Comfortable with billing / EHR software, payer portals, Excel / Google Sheets, and payroll coordination.

• Reliability & Discretion: Consistent, on-time performance and full confidentiality on sensitive participant, payroll, and financial information.

• Home Office Setup: Quiet, private workspace; reliable high-speed internet; a working computer; and a dependable backup plan.

Preferred (Nice-to-Have)

• Prior experience billing for an FMS (Financial Management Services) agency.

• CPB, CPC, or other billing / coding certifications.

• Experience supervising or training other billers.

• Experience cleaning up a billing backlog or rebuilding a billing process from the ground up.

What Success Looks Like

• Clean claims go out on time, every cycle — zero missed timely-filing deadlines.

• Denial rate is low and trending down; recurring denial reasons get fixed at the source.

• AR stays healthy and well-aged; nothing quietly slips past recoverable windows.

• Leadership has clear weekly visibility into billing performance and revenue risk.

• Audits are handled calmly because the documentation trail is already clean.

Compensation & Schedule

Schedule: Monday – Friday, standard business hours (Central Time)

Location: Fully remote

Pay: [Rate to be discussed based on experience]

Start Date: As soon as possible for the right candidate

How to Apply

To apply, please submit the following:

• An updated resume or CV specifically highlighting your home health care billing experience — agencies, years, programs billed (MA, waivers, PCA, CFSS, etc.), and systems used (MN–ITS, EHRs, clearinghouses).

• A short cover letter (3–5 sentences) describing the billing setup you’ve owned before and what you’re proud of in your work — clean claim rate, denial turnaround, backlog cleanup, etc.

• Any relevant certifications (CPB, CPC, etc.).

• Your available start date and confirmation that you can commit to a consistent weekday schedule.

Please do not apply if you do not already have direct home health care billing experience. We’re looking for someone who can walk in, plug in, and run the billing function with confidence from day one.



Robland Home Health Care  •  Aspen FMS

title: Employment Law Paralegal ( US Paralegal Experience Required)
description:
Job Title: Employment Law Paralegal (U.S. Experience Required)

Location: Remote

Work Schedule: Full-time, Pacific Standard Time (PST) | 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM

Rate: $7.00 – $10.00 per hour (depending on experience)



Job Overview

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Employment Law Paralegal with hands-on experience in U.S. employment law. This is a remote position open to qualified candidates who have directly supported U.S.-based employers, law firms, or legal teams handling employment-related matters.

Important: Applicants must clearly declare the employer or organization where they gained their U.S. employment law experience.

Being a licensed lawyer in the Philippines is a strong advantage, but not required.



Key Responsibilities

Assist attorneys with U.S. employment law matters, including but not limited to:

Wrongful termination

Workplace discrimination and harassment

Wage and hour disputes

Employee contracts and compliance

Draft, review, and organize legal documents such as pleadings, motions, and correspondence

Conduct legal research on U.S. federal and state employment laws

Manage case files, deadlines, and documentation

Communicate with clients, witnesses, and external parties as needed

Support case preparation for hearings, mediations, and trials

Maintain confidentiality and ensure accuracy in all legal documentation



Qualifications

Mandatory: Proven experience in U.S. Employment Law (must specify employer where experience was obtained)

Bachelor’s degree in any field (required); coursework or training in legal studies, political science, or related fields is preferred

Strong understanding of U.S. labor and employment regulations

Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

High attention to detail and strong organizational skills

Ability to work independently in a remote setup

Must be available to work 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST

Preferred:

Law degree (Philippines)

title: Copywriter / Thought Leadership Writer
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled copywriter who can do far more than write polished words. This role is for someone who can think strategically, hold a founder’s voice with precision, and translate complex ideas into clear, compelling messaging across platforms.



This is not a plug-and-play copy role. The right person will know how to shape thought leadership, protect nuance, and support a brand with depth, intelligence, and editorial judgment.

What We’re Looking For:



Strategic Copywriter

 You think like a brand strategist who writes, not just a writer waiting for instructions. You can translate high-level ideas into grounded, clear messaging and understand how copy contributes to long-term brand authority, positioning, and trust.



You know how to:

Turn thought leadership into language that feels clear and resonant

Hold context across multiple offers, platforms, and seasons

Build messaging that strengthens authority over time, not just immediate engagement



Deep Fluency in Female Leadership Language



This role is best suited for a woman who understands the nuances of modern female leadership from lived experience.

You should feel comfortable writing about:



Power without performative masculinity

Authority without arrogance

Identity, discernment, boundaries, and self-trust

The emotional interior of leadership

The tone is not “girlboss,” and it is not corporate. It is grounded, intelligent, and embodied.



Ability to Hold and Protect a Distinct Voice



The right writer must be able to enter an established voice without flattening or diluting it.

You should be able to:

Match cadence, rhythm, and emphasis

Write conversationally without sounding casual

Be bold without becoming aggressive

Be warm without becoming soft

This requires taste, restraint, and strong listening skills.



Strong Long-Form Writing Ability



This is not a short-caption-only role. You should be confident writing and shaping:

 sequences that build over time

Long-form posts, essays, and leadership narratives

Workshop descriptions and progra
ssaging

Thought pieces that require structure, pacing, and discernment

You should also have strong editorial instincts and know what should be said, what should be refined, and what should be left unsaid.



High Emotional Intelligence



This role sits close to the founder’s thinking. You must be able to read between the lines, work from voice notes or partially formed ideas, and handle feedback with maturity.

The right person knows how to:

Ask thoughtful, clarifying questions without over-questioning

Interpret nuance, subtext, and intention

Know when to push an idea further and when to protect the message



Comfort With Abstract Ideas

The work often lives in concepts, frameworks, and invisible architecture. You should enjoy working with ideas that are still forming and helping shape them into language people can immediately recognize themselves in.



Platform Versatility



You should be able to write across:




Instagram

LinkedIn

Sales pages

Workshop and program positioning



Professional Maturity



We need someone who is dependable, discreet, and collaborative. This person should manage deadlines responsibly, hold context well, and understand the level of confidentiality that comes with founder-facing work.



Strong Taste and Editorial Judgment



This is one of the most important parts of the role. You should know:

What not to say

When something is too much

When clarity matters more than cleverness

When simplicity or restraint is more powerful than saying more



Key Responsibilities



-Content

Plan out monthly content calendars

Write LinkedIn and Meta posts tied to the content plan and current offers

Write weekly articles

Turn notes, voice notes, or training transcripts into finished content

Research positioning, trends, current language, relevant examples, and insights to inform future content



-Campaigns and Launches

Create campaign briefs

Write promotional, sales, and fulfillment
s

Write support landing pages and other offer-related copy

Develop swipe copy and launch messaging as needed



-Reporting and Performance

Track
 and content performance, including opens, clicks, engagement, and reach

Gather performance insights regularly

Use data to inform rewrites, content adjustments, and future messaging decisions



-Collaboration and Revisions

Revise
s, posts, and other copy based on feedback

Collaborate closely with the founder or team to shape ideas into finished messaging

Handle ad hoc copy requests as needed



Additional Requirements



Must be available to work during PST hours

Can start part-time, with the opportunity to expand hours as responsibilities grow

Starting rate is $7.50 USD per hour, with potential for an increase after the first month based on performance and fit

Must be comfortable working in a collaborative, founder-facing role that requires discretion, responsiveness, and strong follow-through



Bonus Qualifications

These are not required, but would be strong indicators of fit:

Background in psychology, leadership, coaching, philosophy, literature, or narrative work

Experience writing for founders, thought leaders, or experts

Comfort working behind the scenes through ghostwriting, without needing visibility or credit



Ideal Fit

This role is for someone thoughtful, sharp, emotionally intelligent, and deeply attuned to voice. You are not just producing copy. You are helping shape and protect a founder’s message with care, precision, and depth.



To apply:



Send your resume and sample work to 

Applications that do not meet the requirements will not be considered

title: Experienced Bookkeeper (QuickBooks + Dext Expert)
description:
We are hiring an experienced Bookkeeper on behalf of a Canada-based professional bookkeeping firm that supports growing businesses with clean, accurate, and compliant financials.



This is not an entry-level role.



We are looking for someone who truly understands bookkeeping inside and out—someone who can jump into QuickBooks and Dext confidently, work independently, and maintain high standards with minimal supervision.



If you love clean books, reconciliations, and structured workflows—and you take pride in accuracy—this role is for you.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

- Full-cycle bookkeeping using QuickBooks Online

- Daily and monthly transaction categorization

- Bank and credit card reconciliations

- Managing receipts and documents using Dext

- Reviewing, matching, and validating expenses

- Identifying discrepancies and resolving issues proactively

- Supporting month-end and year-end bookkeeping tasks

- Maintaining organized, audit-ready financial records

- Communicating clearly with the internal team when clarification is needed



What We’re Looking For (Must-Haves)

- Minimum 5 years of hands-on bookkeeping experience

- Expert-level QuickBooks Online knowledge

- Strong experience using Dext 

- Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

- Strong understanding of bookkeeping best practices

- Ability to work independently and manage your own workload

- Reliable internet connection and availability during PST business hours

- Professional written and verbal English communication



Nice-to-Haves (Assets)

- Experience supporting Canadian businesses

- Experience working with accounting firms or bookkeeping agencies

- Familiarity with month-end close processes

- Comfortable working with multiple client files



Why This Role Is a Great Opportunity

- Stable, long-term role with a professional Canadian firm

- Clear processes and expectations

- Opportunity to increase hours based on performance

- Work with a team that values accuracy, accountability, and professionalism

- No micromanagement—your expertise is trusted



How to Apply

- Please include the following in your application:

- A brief summary of your bookkeeping experience

- Your years of experience with QuickBooks Online

- Your experience level with Dext (be specific)

- Examples of bookkeeping tasks you’ve handled independently

- Your availability during PST hours



Only applicants with relevant bookkeeping experience will be considered.
